purpose: This JSON is the complete instruction manual for an AI (Claude, GPT, or any code-generation AI) to build the entire Compliance Tracker SaaS platform from scratch. The AI must follow these steps IN ORDER. Each step produces downloadable files. The AI must NOT skip steps or work ahead.

critical_rules.rule_1_read_first: BEFORE writing ANY code, the AI must read the referenced source files listed in 'source_files' section below. These files contain the complete database schemas, API endpoint specs, module descriptions, and UI wireframes. Without reading them, the AI will produce incorrect code.
critical_rules.rule_2_one_step_at_a_time: The AI MUST complete exactly ONE step at a time. After completing a step, it MUST save all produced files to the paths specified in step.output_files, then verify the files exist, then update step.status to 'completed' in a progress tracker file (compliance_tracker_progress.json), then STOP and wait for the next instruction.
critical_rules.rule_3_never_regenerate: If a step is already marked 'completed' in the progress tracker, the AI MUST skip it and move to the next incomplete step. It must NEVER regenerate or overwrite completed work unless explicitly told to redo that step.
critical_rules.rule_4_save_everything: Every file the AI creates MUST be saved to disk immediately using the Write tool. The AI must NOT hold files in memory. Large responses must be split into multiple Write calls.
critical_rules.rule_5_test_after_each_step: After completing each step, the AI must run the relevant verification command (specified in step.verification_command) and confirm it passes before marking the step complete.
critical_rules.rule_6_if_stuck_move_on: If the AI encounters an error it cannot resolve within 3 attempts, it must: (a) log the error in the progress tracker under step.error_log, (b) add a TODO comment in the relevant file, (c) mark the step as 'blocked' (not 'completed'), and (d) move to the next step. Blocked steps can be revisited later.
critical_rules.rule_7_context_recovery: At the start of every step, the AI must: (a) read compliance_tracker_progress.json to know what's done, (b) read the step's description from THIS JSON, (c) read the referenced source files for that step, (d) check existing code in the monorepo. This ensures the AI can recover context even after losing previous conversation history.
critical_rules.rule_8_monorepo_integrity: The AI must never modify files outside the step's specified scope. If a step says 'create packages/db/src/schema.ts', the AI must NOT also modify apps/web/ files unless explicitly instructed.
critical_rules.rule_9_no_hallucination: The AI must only create APIs, tables, and features that are specified in the source files. If the AI thinks something is missing, it should add a TODO comment, not invent new specs.
critical_rules.rule_10_file_size_limit: If any single file exceeds 500 lines, the AI should split it into smaller modules. This keeps code maintainable and avoids write failures.

anti_loop_system.description: This system prevents the AI from going into infinite loops or repeating work.

error_log: list of error messages if blocked

loop_detection.if_same_error_3_times: Mark step as 'blocked', log error, move to next step
loop_detection.if_step_takes_more_than_3_attempts: Mark as 'blocked', add TODO, move on
loop_detection.never_repeat_completed_step: Check progress tracker BEFORE starting any step

checkpoint_system.description: After every 5 steps, the AI must create a checkpoint summary
checkpoint_contains: list of all files created so far
any decisions made that deviate from spec

source_files.description: These files MUST be read by the AI before starting work. They contain the complete project specification.

monorepo_structure.description: The entire project lives in a single monorepo. The AI must create this exact structure.

packages/.db/: Drizzle ORM schemas, migrations, shared DB utilities
packages/.api-client/: Shared API client (used by web and mobile)

development_steps.title: Initialize Monorepo with Turborepo
development_steps.description: Create the monorepo root with Turborepo, npm workspaces, and all workspace directories. Do NOT install all dependencies yet — just the root config.
detailed_instructions: Create root directory: compliance-tracker/
Initialize git: git init
Create turbo.json with pipeline: build, dev, lint, test, type-check
Create root package.json with workspaces: ['apps/*', 'packages/*']
Create apps/web/ directory with package.json (name: @compliancetrack/web)
Create apps/mobile/ directory with package.json (name: @compliancetrack/mobile)
Create packages/db/, packages/types/, packages/api-client/, packages/ui/ directories each with their own package.json
Create supabase/ directory with config.toml (placeholder)
Create .gitignore (node_modules, .next, .expo, dist, .env*, *.log)
Create vercel.json with rewrite rules for API routes
Do NOT run npm install yet
development_steps.verification_command: cd compliance-tracker && cat turbo.json && ls -la apps/ packages/ supabase/
development_steps.success_criteria: All directories exist, turbo.json is valid JSON, all package.json files present

development_steps.title: Install Core Dependencies for All Workspaces
development_steps.description: Install TypeScript, Next.js 15, Expo, Drizzle, Supabase client, and all core dependencies. This step sets up package.json files with correct versions.
detailed_instructions: In packages/db/package.json: add drizzle-orm, drizzle-kit, postgres, @types/node
In packages/types/package.json: add typescript, zod as dependencies
In packages/api-client/package.json: add @tanstack/react-query, ky (HTTP client), zod
In packages/ui/package.json: add react, react-native, @radix-ui/react-slot, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge

In apps/web/package.json: add next@15, react@19, react-dom@19, @supabase/supabase-js, @supabase/ssr, tailwindcss@4, @tailwindcss/postcss, zustand, @tanstack/react-query, @hookform/resolvers, react-hook-form, zod, recharts, @tiptap/react, @tiptap/starter-kit, lucide-react, date-fns, resend, @ai-sdk/anthropic, ai
In apps/mobile/package.json: add expo-router, expo, react-native, react, @supabase/supabase-js, zustand, @tanstack/react-query, expo-notifications, expo-document-picker, expo-camera, date-fns

Verify all installs succeed with no errors
If any package conflicts, resolve by adjusting versions

development_steps.title: Supabase Migration 001 - Core Tables
development_steps.description: Generate and apply the first SQL migration creating core tables: organisations, users, sessions, access_requests, departments. Use 'npx drizzle-kit generate' then apply. Read file_1 step 4 for exact column specs.

Review the generated SQL in supabase/migrations/

Verify the SQL creates: organisations (with unique slug, parent_org_id FK), users (with org_id FK, unique org_id+email), sessions (with user_id FK, ip_address, machine_id), access_requests, departments (with parent_id self-ref)
Verify all tables have: id UUID PK (gen_random_uuid), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ
Verify users table has passcode_hash VARCHAR(255), passcode_salt VARCHAR(64), role with default 'viewer'
Do NOT apply to Supabase yet (no Supabase project connected) — just generate the migration file
The migration file path should be: supabase/migrations/XXXXXX_001_core_tables.sql
Also create a seed.sql with sample data: 1 org, 1 admin user, 6 default departments (Finance, IT, HR, Legal, Operations, Import/Export)

Verify tables created: compliances (with slug UNIQUE, org_id FK, department_id FK, owner_id FK, status enum, priority enum, external_references JSONB), compliance_history (field_name, old_value, new_value, changed_by FK, ip_address, machine_id), comments (compliance_id FK, parent_comment_id self-ref, body TEXT), audit_log (org_id FK, user_id nullable, is_ai_action boolean, ai_model nullable), audit_points, notifications, ai_usage, documents

development_steps.success_criteria: Migration creates all 8 feature tables with correct columns and indexes

development_steps.title: Supabase Migration 003 - RLS Policies
development_steps.description: Create migration with Row Level Security policies for ALL tables. Read file_1 step 6 (M03) for RLS policy specs. Every table (except audit_log inserts) must have RLS enabled.
detailed_instructions: Create manual SQL migration (not generated): supabase/migrations/0003_003_rls_policies.sql
Enable RLS on ALL tables: ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY
Create a helper function: get_current_org_id() that reads from JWT claims or session variable
For each table, create SELECT policy: USING (org_id = get_current_org_id())
For tables users can write to: create INSERT policy WITH CHECK, UPDATE policy USING+WITH CHECK
For audit_log: SELECT only (no INSERT/UPDATE from RLS — service key does inserts)

For multi-client: create policies that allow parent org admins to SELECT from child orgs (WHERE org_id = get_current_org_id() OR org_id IN (SELECT id FROM organisations WHERE parent_org_id = get_current_org_id()))
Test policy logic with EXPLAIN ANALYZE comments in SQL
development_steps.verification_command: cd compliance-tracker && grep -c 'CREATE POLICY' supabase/migrations/*003*
development_steps.success_criteria: At least 30 CREATE POLICY statements (multiple per table)

Create packages/api-client/src/endpoints/auth.ts - Auth API functions: sendPasscode, verifyPasscode, requestMagicLink, logout, getSession, submitAccessRequest — matching the 6 endpoints in file_1 step 5
Create packages/api-client/src/endpoints/compliance.ts - Compliance API functions for CRUD + status change + reassign — matching file_1 step 8
Create packages/api-client/src/endpoints/organisation.ts - Org API functions — matching file_1 step 6
Create packages/api-client/src/index.ts - re-export client and all endpoint functions
Each endpoint function must be fully typed with Zod-validated request params and typed responses using types from packages/types
development_steps.verification_command: cd compliance-tracker && npx tsc --project packages/api-client/tsconfig.json --noEmit

development_steps.title: Create Auth Middleware + JWT Utilities
development_steps.description: Create server-side auth middleware for Next.js API routes. This handles JWT verification, session validation, tenant context extraction, and IP/machine ID capture. Read file_1 step 5 (M02) for auth flow and step 6 (M03) for tenant context middleware.
detailed_instructions: Create apps/web/lib/auth/jwt.ts - JWT sign and verify functions using jose library. Payload includes: org_id, user_id, role, permissions array. 24h expiry for access token, 7d for refresh token.

Create apps/web/lib/auth/session.ts - Session management: createSession (insert into sessions table with IP, machine_id, user_agent), validateSession (check session exists and not expired), destroySession

Create apps/web/lib/auth/middleware.ts - Next.js middleware.ts for: extract JWT from Authorization header or cookie, verify JWT, extract org_id, set request headers (x-org-id, x-user-id, x-user-role), redirect to /login if invalid

Create apps/web/lib/auth/rbac.ts - Permission checker: canUser(user, action, resource) function that checks role permissions matrix from file_1 step 7 (M04). Must check permission_scopes table for per-entity overrides.
Create apps/web/lib/auth/audit-logger.ts - Audit logging utility: logAction(org_id, user_id, action_type, entity_type, entity_id, details, ip, machine_id, is_ai). This inserts into audit_log table.
Machine ID generation: use a hash of User-Agent + screen resolution + timezone as a fingerprint
development_steps.verification_command: cd compliance-tracker && npx tsc --noEmit --project apps/web/tsconfig.json 2>&1 | head -20
development_steps.success_criteria: All auth utilities compile, JWT sign/verify works, middleware exported
